title: Fragrant Rich French Mushroom Stew
description: A resinous and juicy French stew featuring kombu and fermented flavors, perfect for a sick day, made with satvik-friendly ingredients. This dish is inspired by traditional Burgundy cuisine.

ingredients.name: mushrooms
ingredients.notes: such as cremini, shiitake, or a combination

ingredients.name: red wine
ingredients.notes: such as Burgundy or Pinot Noir

instructions: Rinse the kombu sheets and soak them in water for at least 30 minutes.
Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat and saute the chopped onion until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chopped carrots and celery and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown.
Add the red wine, vegetable broth, and soaked kombu, bringing the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the fermented tomato paste and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
